Supercut · creative playbook

protein powder

What the longest-running paid social video ads in this market keep doing — counted, with the footage to prove it.

2026-07-12 · 52 ads studied across 41 brands

How we picked these ads

Advertisers kill losing ads fast, so an ad still delivering after a month has earned a look. We searched 176 ads in this market and kept the ones running 30+ days and seen in the last 30. An AI watched each surviving ad and answered a fixed set of questions about it; code then counted the answers. 10 ads that turned out to sell something else were rejected — they are listed at the bottom. No single ad is called a winner here. The findings are patterns that repeat across 52 ads from 41 different brands.

What the studied ads sell

protein powder12 of 52
whey protein7 of 52
clear protein powder3 of 52
whey protein isolate3 of 52
collagen protein2 of 52
collagen powder2 of 52
23 one-off segments23 of 52

Top takeaways

41 of 52Prioritize creator-led content over branded visual production.
42 of 52Feature the product on screen within the first three seconds.
51 of 52Use text and visuals so the message works without sound.
30 of 52Open with a bold claim or a strong visual hook to stop the scroll.
  1. Address a problem with an energetic creator ugc problem-solution · 21 ads
  2. Build trust with social proof and product demos creator testimonial · 12 ads
  3. Use fast cuts to show the product in a daily routine lifestyle montage · 8 ads
  4. Hook viewers with visual preparation shots product demo · 7 ads
  5. Use bold claims on simple brand graphics still image with text · 3 ads
  6. What to test next
  7. What to double-check

Chapter 1 · ugc problem-solution

Address a problem with an energetic creator

21 ads 19 brands 197 median days live 3 median cuts in first 10s

This is the most common format in the market. It uses personal stories to explain why a specific powder is the solution to a daily struggle.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

bold claim9 of 21
social proof4 of 21
offer or discount2 of 21
problem callout2 of 21
visual hook2 of 21
curiosity tease1 of 21
question1 of 21

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds14 of 21
3 to 10 seconds7 of 21

How they ask for the click

soft12 of 21
hard6 of 21
none3 of 21

How to shoot it

  • Hire a creator to film in a home or gym setting. (20 of 21 ads)
  • Keep the energy high throughout the clip. (16 of 21 ads)
  • Show the full process of using or mixing the product. (16 of 21 ads)
  • Include the product in the shot during the first three seconds. (14 of 21 ads)
  • Use a soft call to action like ‘check it out’. (12 of 21 ads)

Examples that lasted

Mila finds
Opens with: “I'm almost 50 and the reason that I look 18” · 609 days live · 5.4M plays
Serena Neel
Opens with: “So a lot of people are confused why I bought a hundred orange juices.” · 518 days live · 10.0M plays
hauskris
Opens with: “A lot of people say that their protein shake recipe tastes like a milkshake” · 400 days live · 2.7M plays

Watch out

Only 1 out of 21 ads in this category uses a curiosity tease as a hook.

Chapter 2 · creator testimonial

Build trust with social proof and product demos

12 ads 12 brands 175 median days live 1 median cuts in first 10s

Testimonials focus on the results and feelings of the user. They rely on the creator's face and voice to build immediate credibility.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

social proof4 of 12
bold claim3 of 12
problem callout2 of 12
curiosity tease1 of 12
other1 of 12
visual hook1 of 12

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds11 of 12
3 to 10 seconds1 of 12

How they ask for the click

none8 of 12
soft3 of 12
hard1 of 12

How to shoot it

  • Present the product immediately at the start. (11 of 12 ads)
  • Ensure the content feels like a real creator post. (11 of 12 ads)
  • Set a medium pace for the edit. (10 of 12 ads)
  • Use an energetic or sincere emotional tone. (9 of 12 ads)
  • Omit a verbal call to action at the end. (8 of 12 ads)

Examples that lasted

nutribullet
Opens with: “Let's make my favorite protein green smoothie with the Nutribullet Pro 900 blender.” · 910 days live · 24.3M plays
GG la colocha
Opens with: “Why I switched the famous collagen you see here on Tiktok to this one” · 626 days live · 11.0M plays
Miranda | TikTok Shop Finds
Opens with: “How not get scammed on tiktok shop” · 638 days live · 2.2M plays

Watch out

Only 1 ad in this group requires sound to be understood.

Chapter 3 · lifestyle montage

Use fast cuts to show the product in a daily routine

8 ads 7 brands 196 median days live 9 median cuts in first 10s

These ads use many quick clips to show how the powder fits into an active life. They are highly visual and rely on movement.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

visual hook6 of 8
curiosity tease1 of 8
bold claim1 of 8

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds7 of 8
3 to 10 seconds1 of 8

How they ask for the click

soft5 of 8
hard3 of 8

How to shoot it

  • Aim for approximately 9 cuts in the first 10 seconds. (8 of 8 ads)
  • Maintain high energy with fast-paced editing. (7 of 8 ads)
  • Start with a visual hook rather than speech. (6 of 8 ads)
  • Feature creator footage instead of brand studio shots. (6 of 8 ads)
  • Show glimpses of the product rather than a full demo. (5 of 8 ads)

Examples that lasted

Clean Simple Eats
Opens with: “PROTEIN POWDER TOASTED MARSHMALLOW” · 204 days live · 3.8M plays
albert_cancook
227 days live · 7.2M plays
Yovana Mendoza
Opens with: “What I eat in a day 35 yo mom of 2” · 212 days live · 142k plays

Watch out

Only 1 ad out of 8 uses a calm emotional tone.

Chapter 4 · product demo

Hook viewers with visual preparation shots

7 ads 5 brands 93 median days live 8 median cuts in first 10s

Demo ads focus on the texture and mixability of the powder. They often show the product being stirred, shaken, or blended.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

visual hook5 of 7
social proof1 of 7
question1 of 7

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds6 of 7
later1 of 7

How they ask for the click

none4 of 7
soft2 of 7
hard1 of 7

How to shoot it

  • Center the first few seconds on a visual hook. (5 of 7 ads)
  • Include a full demonstration of the product in use. (4 of 7 ads)
  • Use a fast editing pace to keep interest. (4 of 7 ads)
  • Keep the footage energetic and lively. (6 of 7 ads)
  • Skip the final call to action. (4 of 7 ads)

Examples that lasted

albert_cancook
Opens with: “Protein Chocolate Crack for your Valentine” · 219 days live · 1.6M plays
MuscleSport
Opens with: “FAT METABOLIZING” · 232 days live · 49k plays
Favorite Finds
Opens with: “Testing Ninja's Newest Product” · 196 days live · 31k plays

Watch out

Only 1 out of 7 demo ads uses a hard call to action.

Chapter 5 · still image with text

Use bold claims on simple brand graphics

3 ads 2 brands 164 median days live 0 median cuts in first 10s
Only 3 ads fit this format — read this chapter as a hint, not a rule.

This format is rare but effective for simple messaging. It uses large text on a static image to deliver a single point.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

bold claim3 of 3

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds3 of 3

How they ask for the click

soft2 of 3
hard1 of 3

How to shoot it

  • Lead with a bold claim as the main text. (3 of 3 ads)
  • Show the product clearly on the image. (3 of 3 ads)
  • Maintain a branded, professional design feel. (3 of 3 ads)
  • Use a neutral emotional tone. (2 of 3 ads)
  • Keep the presentation slow and readable. (2 of 3 ads)

Examples that lasted

Sprouts Farmers Market
Opens with: “MYTH #10: PROTEIN ONLY COMES FROM MEAT” · 163 days live · 81k plays
Transparent Labs
Opens with: “14 Tasty Flavors” · 173 days live · 21k plays

Watch out

None of these ads include a product demonstration.

What to test next

Social Proof Hook Test

Hypothesis
If we open with a social proof hook in a problem-solution ad, we will increase view time.
Why this test
Social proof is used in 9 ads across the pool but only 4 times in the popular problem-solution format.
Call it on
Average watch time
Shot notes
  • Start with a 'Best rated' overlay
  • Show a creator reading a comment
  • Transition immediately to the product problem
Risk
The social proof might feel less authentic than the problem callout.
Draft script
Everyone is talking about this whey isolate. I used to hate the chalky taste of protein, but this changed everything. Click to see the flavors.

Hard CTA in Problem-Solution

Hypothesis
If we use a hard CTA instead of a soft one, we will increase the click-through rate.
Why this test
Most problem-solution ads use soft CTAs (12 of 21), while hard CTAs are only found in 6 ads.
Call it on
Click-through rate
Shot notes
  • End with a clear 'Shop Now' button
  • Show the product packaging clearly
  • Ensure the creator points toward the link
Risk
The hard sell might turn off viewers who prefer the organic feel of UGC.
Draft script
This is the solution to your post-workout hunger. Stop settling for bad protein. Go to our site and buy yours today.

Question Hook in Demo Ads

Hypothesis
If we use a question hook in a product demo, we will increase engagement.
Why this test
Visual hooks dominate demo ads (5 of 7), while questions are only used once.
Call it on
Engagement rate
Shot notes
  • Open with a text question like 'Still clumpy?'
  • Immediately show the powder dissolving perfectly
  • Keep the pace fast with 8 cuts in 10 seconds
Risk
A question might delay the visual payoff that demo viewers want.
Draft script
Tired of your protein not mixing? Look at how smooth this isolate blends. No clumps, just flavor. Try it out.

What to double-check

Everything above comes from an AI watching ads one at a time and code counting its answers. Here is where that process was noisy, and what it threw out.

Ads rejected as off-market (10)

The search also returned ads selling something else. They were dropped before any counting, so they do not touch the numbers above.

AdvertiserWhy it was dropped
CAYLEYXOoff-market: sells "A meal planning app featuring high protein recipes."
Emmaoff-market: sells "A nutrition and fitness tracking mobile application."
Stealth Health Lifeoff-market: sells "A series of digital cookbooks featuring high-protein, macro-friendly meal prep recipes."
Serenity Kids Baby Foodoff-market: sells "A2 protein-based whole milk toddler formula and matching snacks."
Biotiful Gut Healthoff-market: sells "High-protein kefir yogurt and drinks from Biotiful Gut Health."
BobbyParrishoff-market: sells "This ad sells Purely Elizabeth Cinnamon Raisin Almond Superfood Cereal available at Costco."
Berry Streetoff-market: sells "Professional nutrition and dietitian coaching services covered by insurance."
Kelsey Oppenheimoff-market: sells "High-protein frozen Greek yogurt pints from the brand Yasso, available at Target."
PopCornersoff-market: sells "High protein popped corn snacks in multiple flavors."
Prep Kitchenoff-market: sells "A prepared meal delivery service providing macro-calculated meals."

Ads we could not analyze (3)

These never reached the questionnaire, so they are absent from every count.

AdvertiserWhat went wrong
conspiracycabinetvideo download failed (curl exit 22)
Amy Nguyen 🤍video download failed (curl exit 22)
Shyann Dunlapvideo download failed (curl exit 22)

How reliable was the watching?

We measured the editing pace of every ad twice: once by the AI's eye, once by scene-detection software. The AI was off by 2.3 cuts on average, and within 2 cuts of the software on 60% of ads. The cut counts printed in the chapters are the software's, not the AI's.

Where a spoken hook could be checked against the ad library's own transcript (18 ads), the AI's quote matched 18 times and disagreed 0 times. The other 34 ads had on-screen-text hooks, no hook, or no transcript — nothing to check against.

The AI reported firm format labels for every ad in the pool.

Assumptions this report makes

  • "Still delivering" means the ad was seen in the ad library within the last 30 days. The data offers no direct "currently live" flag.
  • Days-running and play counts come from the ad library and are taken at face value.
  • A long-running ad earned study, but nothing here proves any single ad was profitable. Only the repeated patterns are conclusions.
  • Spoken quotes were transcribed by the AI from the audio; where the ad library had its own transcript, we cross-checked (see above).

Study ranking: ads are ordered for the example lists by a score of longevity ×0.5 + engagement ×0.3 + formatConfidence ×0.2, each part normalized within this pool, with at most one ad per brand in any list.

Made with Supercut. Days-running and play counts come from the ad library data; "still delivering" means seen in the last 30 days, the closest signal the data offers to "currently live".
